docs.title: ISWAT Team S2-06 Meeting in Oslo
docs.text: The International Space Weather Action Teams (ISWAT) S2-06 Meeting will take place in the week of September 21-25, 2026, in Oslo, Norway.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
As a major energy input to the terrestrial upper atmosphere, the solar spectral irradiance is an important driver of space weather. Variability on timescales of one to several days affects the drag experienced by orbiting objects and the propagation of radio signals via scintillation and a changing plasma frequency altitude profile. Such variability is associated with the evolution of solar active regions and the rotation of active regions onto and off of the visible solar disk.

The goal of this meeting is to provide physical models for improved forecasts of the spectral irradiance and accurate nowcasts of gaps in wavelength coverage.

docs.title: Topics
docs.text:
The meeting will cover a wide range of topics, including but not limited to:

  • Analysis, modeling and prediction of coronal structure and solar wind sources.
  • Understanding coronal heating and solar wind acceleration.
  • Quantifying and forecasting irradiance inputs to ionosphere/thermosphere models.
Photo credit: Oscar Daniel Rangel on Unsplash

docs.title: Schedule
docs.text: Below is the schedule for the ISWAT Team Meeting. It will be updated as we firm up the details.

docs.title: Meeting Location
docs.text:
The ISWAT Action Team meeting will be held at the Institute of Theoretical Astrophysics (ITA), University of Oslo, Norway.

Photo credit: University of Oslo

docs.title: Accommodation
docs.text: Hotel recommendations will be provided closer to the meeting date. Please check back soon.

docs.title: Transportation
docs.text: Oslo has a reliable and well-connected public transport system (metro, tram, bus, and train). Download the Ruter app to plan routes and buy tickets. Tickets are valid across all modes of transport within the city. For most participants, a 7-day Zone 1 ticket will be the most convenient option.

docs.title: From Oslo Airport (OSL) to the City
docs.text: The easiest option is the regional train (lines RE10, RE11, or R12) to National Theatre station, which takes approximately 45 minutes. Purchase a Zone 1–4N ticket in the Ruter app. If arriving late at night, the Flytoget express train runs until 00:50, and the Flybussen bus service runs even later.

docs.title: From the City Center to the Venue
docs.text:
Take the T-bane (metro) lines 4 or 5 in the direction of Sognsvann or Vestli and get off at Blindern station. The ride takes around 15 minutes from the city center.

[!TIP] Google Maps works for Oslo transit, but the Ruter app has more accurate real-time schedules.
